### Set E2B Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.6/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for an E2B sandbox. This method can extend or reduce the existing timeout. The maximum timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. It requires the timeout duration in seconds and optionally accepts a request timeout. This is an overload method.

```python
from typing import Optional

@overload
def set_timeout(timeout: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """Set the timeout of the sandbox."""
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets or updates the timeout for the current sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically killed once the timeout expires. This method can be used to extend or reduce the existing timeout. It requires the new timeout value in seconds and optionally accepts a request timeout.

```python
await sandbox.set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout using E2B SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

This Python function enables setting a timeout for a sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically killed once the timeout expires. It accepts a timeout duration in seconds and can extend or reduce existing timeouts. The maximum timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. Dependencies include ApiParams.

```python
from typing import Unpack
from e2b.api_base import ApiParams


@overload
def set_timeout(timeout: int, **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> None:
    # Set the timeout of the sandbox.
    # Arguments:
    # - timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**
    pass


@overload
@staticmethod
def set_timeout(sandbox_id: str, timeout: int,
                **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> None:
    # Set the timeout of the sandbox specified by sandbox ID.
    # Arguments:
    # - sandbox_id: Sandbox ID
    # - timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**
    pass


@class_method_variant("_cls_set_timeout")
def set_timeout(timeout: int, **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> None:
    # Set the timeout of the sandbox.
    # Arguments:
    # - timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript Optional Timeout Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/filesystem/page.mdx

Defines an optional timeout in milliseconds for watch operations. A value of 0 disables the timeout. The default timeout is 60 seconds.

```typescript
optional timeoutMs: number;

// Default: 60_000 // 60 seconds
```

--------------------------------

### Define TimeoutError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when a timeout error occurs during sandbox operations or requests. This error class distinguishes between different timeout causes like sandbox timeouts, request timeouts, and command execution timeouts. It takes a string message.

```typescript
class TimeoutError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TimeoutError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Set Timeout for Current Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.6/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for the current sandbox. This function can be used to extend or reduce the sandbox's timeout. The maximum allowed timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. It takes the timeout duration in seconds as a required argument and an optional request timeout.

```python
from typing import Optional

# Assuming 'overload' is imported from 'typing'

@overload
async def set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """Set the timeout of the sandbox.

    After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed.
    This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

    Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

    Args:
        timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**
        request_timeout: Timeout for the request in **seconds**
    """
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Set timeout for a specified sandbox instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.7.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets or updates the timeout for a specified sandbox identified by its ID. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. This method can extend or reduce the existing timeout. The maximum allowed timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. Supports API key, domain, debug, request timeout, and proxy settings.

```python
await AsyncSandbox.set_timeout(
    sandbox_id: str,
    timeout: int,
    api_key: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
    debug: Optional[bool] = None,
    request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
    proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None
) -> None
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Configures the automatic timeout for a sandbox instance. After the specified duration, the sandbox will be automatically terminated. This can extend or reduce existing timeouts. The maximum duration is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. Requires the timeout duration in seconds and optionally a request timeout.

```python
@overload
async def set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets or updates the timeout for a specified sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically terminated once the timeout expires. This function allows for both extending and reducing the existing timeout. Maximum timeouts are 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

```python
await e2b.set_timeout(sandbox_id="your-sandbox-id", timeout=3600)
```

--------------------------------

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Adjusts the timeout duration for a sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically terminated once the set timeout expires. This method can either extend or reduce the initial timeout. The maximum allowed timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

```python
async def set_timeout(timeout: int, **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> None
```

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.2/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets or updates the timeout for the current sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. This method can adjust the sandbox's lifespan. It takes the timeout duration in seconds and an optional request timeout.

```python
@overload
async def set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """
    Set the timeout of the sandbox.

    After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed.
    This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

    Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

    **Arguments**:
        - timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**
        - request_timeout: Timeout for the request in **seconds**
    """
    pass
```

### Sandbox Timeout Management

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.2/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Manage the timeout settings for a sandbox. This allows you to extend or reduce the existing timeout. The maximum allowed timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/set-timeout

### Description
Sets the timeout for a specific sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. This method can be used to adjust the timeout from its initial creation or the last set timeout.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/set-timeout`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandbox_id** (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **timeout** (integer) - Required - The desired timeout for the sandbox in seconds. Maximum is 86400 for Pro users and 3600 for Hobby users.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "timeout": 3600
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Confirmation message.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Sandbox timeout updated successfully."
}
```
```

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes/set-timeout

### Description
Sets the timeout for the current sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. This method can be used to adjust the timeout from its initial creation or the last set timeout.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/sandboxes/set-timeout`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **timeout** (integer) - Required - The desired timeout for the sandbox in seconds. Maximum is 86400 for Pro users and 3600 for Hobby users.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "timeout": 3600
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Confirmation message.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Sandbox timeout updated successfully."
}
```
```

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.5/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Allows setting or extending the timeout for a sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically killed when the timeout expires. Maximum sandbox lifespan is restricted to 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. Arguments include the timeout duration in seconds and an optional request timeout.

```python
async def set_timeout(timeout: int,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

```python
async def set_timeout(sandbox_id: str,
                      timeout: int,
                      api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                      domain: Optional[str] = None,
                      debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                      request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None
```

### Set Sandbox Timeout (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Adjusts the timeout for a sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically terminated once the specified timeout expires. This method can be used to dynamically extend or reduce the sandbox's lifecycle. It accepts the timeout duration in seconds and an optional request timeout.

```python
@overload
async def set_timeout(timeout: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """
    Set the timeout of the sandbox.

    After the timeout expires the sandbox will be automatically killed.
    This method can extend or reduce the sandbox timeout set when creating the sandbox or from the last call to `.set_timeout`.

    Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.

    Arguments:
        timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**
        request_timeout: Timeout for the request in **seconds**
    """
    pass
```

### Set Sandbox Timeout in Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Sets the timeout for a specified sandbox. The sandbox will be automatically killed after the timeout expires. This can be used to extend or reduce the existing timeout. The maximum timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

```python
import asyncio
from typing import Optional, List
from datetime import datetime

from e2b.api_client import ApiParams
from e2b.sandbox.sandbox import SandboxInfo, SandboxMetrics


@overload
@staticmethod
async def set_timeout(sandbox_id: str, timeout: int,
                      **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> None

@class_method_variant("_cls_set_timeout")
async def set_timeout(timeout: int, **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> None


@overload
async def get_info(**opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> SandboxInfo

@overload
@staticmethod
async def get_info(sandbox_id: str, **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> SandboxInfo

@class_method_variant("_cls_get_info")
async def get_info(**opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> SandboxInfo


@overload
async def get_metrics(start: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      end: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> List[SandboxMetrics]

@overload
@staticmethod
async def get_metrics(sandbox_id: str,
                      start: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      end: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> List[SandboxMetrics]

@class_method_variant("_cls_get_metrics")
async def get_metrics(start: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      end: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None,
                      **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> List[SandboxMetrics]
```

Sets the timeout for the current sandbox instance. The sandbox will be automatically killed after the specified timeout in seconds. This method can adjust the existing timeout. It takes the timeout duration and an optional request timeout. Maximum sandbox lifetime is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

```python
@overload
def set_timeout(timeout: int, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> None:
    """Set the timeout of the sandbox."""
    # Implementation details...
```

--------------------------------

### Set timeout for a sandbox using Sandbox.setTimeout

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This static method allows you to set or update the timeout for a specific sandbox. It takes the sandbox ID and the timeout duration in milliseconds. This can be used to extend or reduce the existing sandbox timeout. The maximum allowed timeout is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users.

```typescript
await Sandbox.setTimeout('sandbox-id', 60000) // Set timeout to 1 minute

```
